title: Runtime-governed coexistence
summary: Multiple versions still exist, but the runtime now resolves compatibility and version selection through explicit rules and contract lineage.

services.summary: Legacy version retained for older consumers under explicit compatibility rules.
Primary governed version selected for newer consumers by runtime policy.
Pinned to the legacy contract line until migration is complete.

runtime_decisions: Selected v1 only for consumers that explicitly declare legacy compatibility.
Selected v2 as the default governed path for new consumers.
Rejected ambiguous selection by requiring contract-line metadata during resolution.
expectations: Multiple versions can coexist without fragmentation when compatibility is defined behaviorally and enforced at runtime.
The runtime restores coherence by resolving interactions through explicit rules instead of local conventions.
Chapter 11's core point is visible here: change is continuous, but fragmentation is optional.
